1→# External Agents for Entire CLI
     2→
     3→This repository contains standalone external agent binaries that extend the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli) with support for additional AI coding agents.
     4→
     5→## What Are External Agents?
     6→
     7→External agents are standalone binaries (named `entire-agent-<name>`) that teach Entire CLI how to work with AI coding agents it doesn't natively support. When an external agent is installed on your `PATH`, Entire discovers it automatically and gains the ability to:
     8→
     9→- **Create checkpoints** during AI coding sessions so you can rewind mistakes
    10→- **Capture transcripts** of what the AI agent did and why
    11→- **Install hooks** so the AI agent's lifecycle events (start, stop, commit) flow through Entire
    12→
    13→External agents communicate with Entire CLI via subcommands that accept and return JSON over stdin/stdout. See the [external agent protocol spec](https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md) for the full interface.
    14→
    15→## Available Agents
    16→
    17→| Agent | Directory | Status |
    18→|-------|-----------|--------|
    19→| [Kiro](agents/entire-agent-kiro/) | `agents/entire-agent-kiro/` | Implemented — hooks + transcript analysis |
    20→
    21→## Building a New External Agent
    22→
    23→This repo includes a skill that guides you through building a new external agent using an E2E-first TDD pipeline. The skill runs in three phases:
    24→
    25→1. **Research** — analyzes the target AI agent's file formats, session layout, and hook mechanisms
    26→2. **Write tests** — generates E2E and unit tests against the external agent protocol
    27→3. **Implement** — builds the Go binary to pass all tests
    28→
    29→### Getting Started — Zero Setup
    30→
    31→Clone the repo and open it in your AI coding tool. Each tool auto-discovers the skill with no additional configuration:
    32→
    33→| Tool | How it discovers | What to say |
    34→|------|-----------------|-------------|
    35→| **Claude Code** | `.claude/skills/` directory | `/entire-external-agent` |
    36→| **Codex** | `AGENTS.md` at project root | "Build an external agent" |
    37→| **Cursor** | `.cursor/rules/` directory | "Build an external agent" |
    38→| **OpenCode** | `.opencode/plugins/` auto-loaded | "Build an external agent" |
    39→
    40→The skill files live in `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/` if you want to read the details.
    41→
    42→## E2E Tests
    43→
    44→The `e2e/` directory contains a shared test harness that exercises all external agents. Tests are split into two tiers:
    45→
    46→- **Subcommand tests** (`kiro_test.go`) — exercise each protocol subcommand directly against the agent binary (identity, sessions, transcript, hooks, transcript analysis). These run without any external dependencies beyond the agent binary itself.
    47→- **Lifecycle tests** (`kiro_lifecycle_test.go`) — exercise the full integration flow: `entire enable`, agent prompt execution, git commit, checkpoint creation, and rewind. These require the `entire` CLI and the agent's own CLI (e.g. `kiro-cli-chat`) to be available.
    48→
    49→### Running Tests
    50→
    51→```bash
    52→# Run all E2E tests (subcommand-level only; lifecycle tests skip if deps missing)
    53→make test-e2e
    54→
    55→# Run lifecycle tests (fails instead of skipping if entire/kiro-cli-chat are missing)
    56→make test-e2e-lifecycle
    57→
    58→# Run unit tests for all agents
    59→make test-unit
    60→
    61→# Run everything
    62→make test-all
    63→```
    64→
    65→### Test Harness Architecture
    66→
    67→The shared harness auto-discovers and builds all agents in `agents/` via `TestMain`:
    68→
    69→| File | Purpose |
    70→|------|---------|
    71→| `e2e/setup_test.go` | `TestMain` entry point — discovers agents, builds binaries, configures PATH |
    72→| `e2e/testenv.go` | `TestEnv` — isolated filesystem environment with agent binary runner |
    73→| `e2e/harness.go` | `AgentRunner` — executes agent subcommands, captures stdout/stderr/exit code |
    74→| `e2e/fixtures.go` | Test input builders: `HookInput`, `ParseHookInput`, `KiroTranscript` |
    75→| `e2e/entire.go` | CLI wrappers: `EntireEnable`, `EntireDisable`, `EntireRewindList`, `EntireRewind` |
    76→| `e2e/lifecycle.go` | `LifecycleEnv` — full lifecycle environment (git repo + `entire enable` + checkpoint helpers) |
    77→
    78→### Environment Variables
    79→
    80→| Variable | Description |
    81→|----------|-------------|
    82→| `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` | Path to the `entire` binary (defaults to `entire` from PATH) |
    83→| `E2E_REQUIRE_LIFECYCLE` | Set to `1` to fail (instead of skip) when lifecycle dependencies are missing |
    84→
    85→## Repository Layout
    86→
    87→```
    88→agents/                          # Standalone external agent projects
    89→  entire-agent-kiro/             # Kiro agent (Go binary)
    90→e2e/                             # Shared E2E test harness for all agents
    91→.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/  # Skill files (research, test-writer, implementer)
    92→AGENTS.md                        # Codex auto-discovery
    93→.cursor/rules/                   # Cursor auto-discovery
    94→.opencode/plugins/               # OpenCode auto-discovery
    95→```
    96→

1→//go:build e2e
     2→
     3→package e2e
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"os/exec"
     9→	"path/filepath"
    10→	"runtime"
    11→	"strings"
    12→	"testing"
    13→
    14→	// Import agents package to trigger init() registration.
    15→	_ "github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e/agents"
    16→	"github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e/entire"
    17→	"github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e/testutil"
    18→)
    19→
    20→// agentBinaries maps agent names to their built binary paths.
    21→var agentBinaries = map[string]string{}
    22→
    23→func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
    24→	tmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "e2e-agents-*")
    25→	if err != nil {
    26→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "failed to create temp dir: %v\n", err)
    27→		os.Exit(1)
    28→	}
    29→	defer os.RemoveAll(tmpDir)
    30→
    31→	discoveredAgents, err := discoverAgents()
    32→	if err != nil {
    33→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "failed to discover agents: %v\n", err)
    34→		os.Exit(1)
    35→	}
    36→
    37→	if len(discoveredAgents) == 0 {
    38→		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "no agents found in agents/ directory")
    39→		os.Exit(1)
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	for _, agentDir := range discoveredAgents {
    43→		agentName := filepath.Base(agentDir)
    44→		binPath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, agentName)
    45→		// Each agent has its own go.mod, so build from the agent's directory
    46→		agentAbsDir := filepath.Join(repoRoot(), agentDir)
    47→		mainPkg := "./cmd/" + agentName
    48→
    49→		fmt.Printf("Building %s...\n", agentName)
    50→		cmd := exec.Command("go", "build", "-o", binPath, mainPkg)
    51→		cmd.Dir = agentAbsDir
    52→		cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    53→		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    54→		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
    55→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "failed to build %s: %v\n", agentName, err)
    56→			os.Exit(1)
    57→		}
    58→
    59→		agentBinaries[agentName] = binPath
    60→		fmt.Printf("Built %s -> %s\n", agentName, binPath)
    61→	}
    62→
    63→	// Add the temp bin directory to PATH so that `entire enable` can discover
    64→	// the agent binaries (e.g. entire-agent-kiro) during lifecycle tests.
    65→	os.Setenv("PATH", tmpDir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
    66→
    67→	// --- Artifact directory setup ---
    68→	runDir := os.Getenv("E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR")
    69→	if runDir == "" {
    70→		_, file, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
    71→		testutil.ArtifactRoot = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(file), "artifacts")
    72→		runDir = testutil.ArtifactRunDir()
    73→	}
    74→	_ = os.MkdirAll(runDir, 0o755)
    75→	testutil.SetRunDir(runDir)
    76→
    77→	// Prepend the entire binary's directory to PATH so git hooks resolve
    78→	// to the same binary the test harness uses.
    79→	entireBin := entire.BinPath()
    80→	if dir := filepath.Dir(entireBin); dir != "." {
    81→		os.Setenv("PATH", dir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	// --- Preflight checks ---
    85→	if _, err := exec.LookPath("tmux"); err != nil {
    86→		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "warning: tmux not found — interactive session tests will fail")
    87→	}
    88→
    89→	// Write entire version info to artifact dir.
    90→	version := "unknown"
    91→	if out, err := exec.Command(entireBin, "version").Output(); err == nil {
    92→		version = string(out)
    93→	}
    94→	preflight := fmt.Sprintf("entire binary:  %s\nentire version: %s\n", entireBin, version)
    95→	_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(runDir, "entire-version.txt"), []byte(preflight), 0o644)
    96→
    97→	// Isolate git config to prevent user's ~/.gitconfig from interfering.
    98→	os.Setenv("GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL", "/dev/null")
    99→
   100→	os.Exit(m.Run())
   101→}
   102→
   103→func discoverAgents() ([]string, error) {
   104→	agentsDir := filepath.Join(repoRoot(), "agents")
   105→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(agentsDir)
   106→	if err != nil {
   107→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read agents dir: %w", err)
   108→	}
   109→
   110→	var agentDirs []string
   111→	for _, entry := range entries {
   112→		if !entry.IsDir() {
   113→			continue
   114→		}
   115→		if !strings.HasPrefix(entry.Name(), "entire-agent-") {
   116→			continue
   117→		}
   118→		// Verify it has a cmd/<name>/main.go
   119→		mainFile := filepath.Join(agentsDir, entry.Name(), "cmd", entry.Name(), "main.go")
   120→		if _, err := os.Stat(mainFile); err != nil {
   121→			continue
   122→		}
   123→		agentDirs = append(agentDirs, filepath.Join("agents", entry.Name()))
   124→	}
   125→	return agentDirs, nil
   126→}
   127→
   128→func repoRoot() string {
   129→	// Walk up from e2e/ to find the repo root
   130→	dir, err := os.Getwd()
   131→	if err != nil {
   132→		return ".."
   133→	}
   134→	// If we're in the e2e directory, go up one level
   135→	if filepath.Base(dir) == "e2e" {
   136→		return filepath.Dir(dir)
   137→	}
   138→	return dir
   139→}

1→//go:build e2e
     2→
     3→package e2e
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"context"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"path/filepath"
     9→	"testing"
    10→	"time"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e/entire"
    13→	"github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e/testutil"
    14→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    15→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    16→)
    17→
    18→// TestLifecycle_SinglePromptManualCommit verifies the basic flow:
    19→// agent creates a file → git add + commit → checkpoint exists with trailer.
    20→func TestLifecycle_SinglePromptManualCommit(t *testing.T) {
    21→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    22→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called hello.txt with the content 'hello world'. Do not ask for confirmation.")
    23→		require.NoError(t, err, "prompt failed")
    24→
    25→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "hello.txt")
    26→
    27→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
    28→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "add hello.txt")
    29→
    30→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 30*time.Second)
    31→
    32→		cpID := testutil.AssertHasCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
    33→		testutil.AssertCheckpointExists(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    34→	})
    35→}
    36→
    37→// TestLifecycle_MultiplePromptsManualCommit sends two prompts, commits once,
    38→// and verifies the checkpoint covers both files.
    39→func TestLifecycle_MultiplePromptsManualCommit(t *testing.T) {
    40→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 3*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    41→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called foo.txt containing 'foo'. Do not ask for confirmation.")
    42→		require.NoError(t, err, "first prompt failed")
    43→
    44→		_, err = s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called bar.txt containing 'bar'. Do not ask for confirmation.")
    45→		require.NoError(t, err, "second prompt failed")
    46→
    47→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "foo.txt")
    48→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "bar.txt")
    49→
    50→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
    51→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "add foo and bar")
    52→
    53→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 30*time.Second)
    54→		testutil.AssertCheckpointAdvanced(t, s)
    55→	})
    56→}
    57→
    58→// TestLifecycle_DetectAndEnable verifies that `entire enable --agent kiro`
    59→// works when .kiro/ already exists in the repo.
    60→func TestLifecycle_DetectAndEnable(t *testing.T) {
    61→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 1*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, _ context.Context) {
    62→		// The repo is already set up with entire enable by ForEachAgent/SetupRepo.
    63→		// Verify .entire/ directory exists.
    64→		_, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(s.Dir, ".entire"))
    65→		require.NoError(t, err, "expected .entire/ directory after enable")
    66→	})
    67→}
    68→
    69→// TestLifecycle_HooksInstalledAfterEnable verifies that hooks are installed
    70→// after running `entire enable`.
    71→func TestLifecycle_HooksInstalledAfterEnable(t *testing.T) {
    72→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 1*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, _ context.Context) {
    73→		agentBinName := "entire-agent-" + s.Agent.EntireAgent()
    74→		binPath, ok := agentBinaries[agentBinName]
    75→		if !ok {
    76→			t.Skipf("%s binary not built", agentBinName)
    77→		}
    78→
    79→		runner := &AgentRunner{
    80→			BinaryPath: binPath,
    81→			Env: []string{
    82→				"ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT=" + s.Dir,
    83→				"HOME=" + os.Getenv("HOME"),
    84→				"PATH=" + os.Getenv("PATH"),
    85→				"LANG=en_US.UTF-8",
    86→			},
    87→		}
    88→
    89→		var resp struct {
    90→			Installed bool `json:"installed"`
    91→		}
    92→		runner.RunJSON(t, &resp, "", "are-hooks-installed")
    93→
    94→		assert.True(t, resp.Installed, "hooks should be installed after entire enable")
    95→	})
    96→}
    97→
    98→// TestLifecycle_RewindPreCommit verifies the rewind flow before a commit:
    99→// create file A → commit → snapshot rewind points → create file B → rewind → file A exists, file B gone.
   100→func TestLifecycle_RewindPreCommit(t *testing.T) {
   101→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 3*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
   102→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called alpha.txt with content 'alpha'. Do not ask for confirmation.")
   103→		require.NoError(t, err, "first prompt failed")
   104→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "alpha.txt")
   105→
   106→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
   107→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "add alpha")
   108→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 30*time.Second)
   109→
   110→		points := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
   111→		require.NotEmpty(t, points, "expected at least one rewind point after first commit")
   112→		rewindTarget := points[0].ID
   113→
   114→		_, err = s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called beta.txt with content 'beta'. Do not ask for confirmation.")
   115→		require.NoError(t, err, "second prompt failed")
   116→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "beta.txt")
   117→
   118→		err = entire.Rewind(t, s.Dir, rewindTarget)
   119→		require.NoError(t, err, "rewind failed")
   120→
   121→		_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(s.Dir, "alpha.txt"))
   122→		assert.NoError(t, err, "alpha.txt should exist after rewind")
   123→		_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(s.Dir, "beta.txt"))

   125→	})

   127→
   128→// TestLifecycle_RewindAfterCommit verifies that rewind points are available
   129→// after a git commit and that rewinding restores the correct state.
   130→func TestLifecycle_RewindAfterCommit(t *testing.T) {
   131→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 4*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
   132→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called first.txt with content 'first'. Do not ask for confirmation.")
   133→		require.NoError(t, err, "first prompt failed")
   134→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
   135→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "add first.txt")
   136→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 30*time.Second)
   137→
   138→		pointsAfterFirst := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
   139→		require.NotEmpty(t, pointsAfterFirst, "expected rewind points after first commit")
   140→
   141→		_, err = s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called second.txt with content 'second'. Do not ask for confirmation.")
   142→		require.NoError(t, err, "second prompt failed")
   143→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
   144→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "add second.txt")
   145→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 30*time.Second)
   146→
   147→		pointsAfterSecond := entire.RewindList(t, s.Dir)
   148→		assert.Greater(t, len(pointsAfterSecond), len(pointsAfterFirst),
   149→			"expected more rewind points after second commit")
   150→
   151→		err = entire.Rewind(t, s.Dir, pointsAfterFirst[0].ID)
   152→		require.NoError(t, err, "rewind failed")
   153→
   154→		_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(s.Dir, "first.txt"))
   155→		assert.NoError(t, err, "first.txt should exist after rewind")
   156→		_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(s.Dir, "second.txt"))
   157→		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err), "second.txt should not exist after rewind")
   158→	})
   159→}

   161→// TestLifecycle_SessionPersistence verifies that a session file is created in
   162→// .entire/tmp/ after running a prompt.
   163→func TestLifecycle_SessionPersistence(t *testing.T) {
   164→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
   165→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called session-test.txt with content 'test'. Do not ask for confirmation.")

1→# Debug Entire CLI via E2E Artifacts
     2→
     3→Diagnose Entire CLI bugs using captured artifacts from the E2E test suite. Artifacts are written to `e2e/artifacts/` locally or downloaded from CI via GitHub Actions.
     4→
     5→## Inputs
     6→
     7→The user provides either:
     8→- **A test run directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/` -- triage all failures
     9→- **A specific test directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/{TestName}-{agent}/` -- debug one test
    10→
    11→## Artifact Layout
    12→
    13→```
    14→e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/
    15→├── report.nocolor.txt          # Pass/fail/skip summary with error lines
    16→├── test-events.json            # Raw Go test events (NDJSON)
    17→├── entire-version.txt          # CLI version under test
    18→└── {TestName}-{agent}/
    19→    ├── PASS or FAIL            # Status marker
    20→    ├── console.log             # Full operation transcript
    21→    ├── git-log.txt             # git log --decorate --graph --all
    22→    ├── git-tree.txt            # ls-tree HEAD + checkpoint branch
    23→    ├── entire-logs/entire.log  # CLI structured JSON logs
    24→    ├── checkpoint-metadata/    # Checkpoint + session metadata
    25→    └── repo -> /tmp/...        # Symlink to preserved repo (E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1 only)
    26→```
    27→
    28→## Preserved Repo
    29→
    30→When the test run was executed with `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1`, each test's artifact directory contains a `repo` symlink pointing to the preserved temporary git repository. This is the actual repo the test operated on -- you can inspect it directly.
    31→
    32→**Navigate via the symlink** (e.g., `{artifact-dir}/repo/`) rather than resolving the `/tmp/...` path. The symlink lives inside the artifact directory so permissions and paths stay consistent.
    33→
    34→The preserved repo contains:
    35→- Full git history with all branches (main, `entire/checkpoints/v1`)
    36→- The `.entire/` directory with CLI state, config, and raw logs
    37→- The `.claude/` directory (if Claude Code was the agent)
    38→- All files the agent created or modified, in their final state
    39→
    40→This is the most powerful debugging tool -- you can run `git log`, `git diff`, `git show`, inspect `.entire/` internals, and see exactly what the CLI left behind.
    41→
    42→## Debugging Workflow
    43→
    44→### 1. Triage (if given a run directory)
    45→
    46→Read `report.nocolor.txt` to identify failures and their error messages. Each entry shows the test name, agent, duration, and failure output with file:line references.
    47→
    48→### 2. Read console.log (most important)
    49→
    50→Full transcript of every operation:
    51→- `> claude -p "..." ...` -- agent prompts with stdout/stderr
    52→- `> git add/commit/...` -- git commands
    53→- `> send: ...` -- interactive session inputs
    54→
    55→This tells you what happened chronologically.
    56→
    57→### 3. Read test source code
    58→
    59→Use the file:line from the report to find the test in `e2e/tests/`. Understand what the test expected to happen vs what console.log shows actually happened.
    60→
    61→### 4. Diagnose the CLI behavior
    62→
    63→Cross-reference console.log (what happened) with the test (what should have happened). Focus on CLI-level issues:
    64→
    65→| Symptom | CLI Investigation |
    66→|---------|-------------------|
    67→| Checkpoint not created / timeout | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for hook invocations, phase transitions, errors |
    68→| Wrong checkpoint content | Check `git-tree.txt` for checkpoint branch files, `checkpoint-metadata/` for session info |
    69→| Hooks didn't fire | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for missing hook entries (session-start, user-prompt-submit, stop, post-commit) |
    70→| Stash/unstash problems | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for stash-related log lines, `git-log.txt` for commit ordering |
    71→| Attribution issues | Check `checkpoint-metadata/` for `files_touched`, session metadata for attribution data |
    72→| Strategy mismatch | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for `strategy` field, verify auto-commit vs manual-commit behavior |
    73→
    74→### 5. Deep dive files
    75→
    76→- **entire-logs/entire.log**: Structured JSON logs -- hook lifecycle, session phases (`active` -> `idle` -> `ended`), warnings, errors. Key fields: `component`, `hook`, `strategy`, `session_id`.
    77→- **git-log.txt**: Commit graph showing main branch, `entire/checkpoints/v1`, checkpoint initialization.
    78→- **git-tree.txt**: Files at HEAD vs checkpoint branch (separated by `--- entire/checkpoints/v1 ---`).
    79→- **checkpoint-metadata/**: `metadata.json` has `checkpoint_id`, `strategy`, `files_touched`, `token_usage`, and `sessions` array. Session subdirs have per-session details.
    80→
    81→### 6. Report findings
    82→
    83→Identify whether the issue is in:
    84→- **CLI hooks** (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit)
    85→- **Session management** (phase transitions, session tracking)
    86→- **Checkpoint creation** (branch management, metadata writing)
    87→- **Attribution** (file tracking, prompt correlation)
    88→- **Strategy logic** (auto-commit vs manual-commit behavior)
    89→

1→package testutil

     3→import "time"
     4→
     5→// TokenUsage tracks API token consumption for a checkpoint.
     6→type TokenUsage struct {
     7→	InputTokens         int `json:"input_tokens"`
     8→	CacheCreationTokens int `json:"cache_creation_tokens"`
     9→	CacheReadTokens     int `json:"cache_read_tokens"`
    10→	OutputTokens        int `json:"output_tokens"`
    11→	APICallCount        int `json:"api_call_count"`
    12→}
    13→
    14→// Attribution tracks code authorship for a checkpoint.
    15→type Attribution struct {
    16→	CalculatedAt    time.Time `json:"calculated_at"`
    17→	AgentLines      int       `json:"agent_lines"`
    18→	HumanAdded      int       `json:"human_added"`
    19→	HumanModified   int       `json:"human_modified"`
    20→	HumanRemoved    int       `json:"human_removed"`
    21→	TotalCommitted  int       `json:"total_committed"`
    22→	AgentPercentage float64   `json:"agent_percentage"`
    23→}
    24→
    25→// CheckpointMetadata represents the top-level metadata.json for a checkpoint.
    26→type CheckpointMetadata struct {
    27→	CLIVersion       string       `json:"cli_version"`
    28→	CheckpointID     string       `json:"checkpoint_id"`
    29→	Strategy         string       `json:"strategy"`
    30→	Branch           string       `json:"branch"`
    31→	CheckpointsCount int          `json:"checkpoints_count"`
    32→	FilesTouched     []string     `json:"files_touched"`
    33→	Sessions         []SessionRef `json:"sessions"`
    34→	TokenUsage       TokenUsage   `json:"token_usage"`
    35→}
    36→
    37→// SessionRef is a pointer to a session within checkpoint metadata.
    38→type SessionRef struct {
    39→	Metadata    string `json:"metadata"`

    43→	Prompt      string `json:"prompt"`
    44→}
    45→
    46→// SessionMetadata represents a session's metadata.json within a checkpoint.

# E2E Tests

End-to-end tests for external agents, exercising the full lifecycle: agent prompts, git hooks, checkpoints, and rewind.

## Structure

```
e2e/
├── agents/           # Agent interface + implementations (kiro, etc.)
│   ├── agent.go      # Agent/Session interfaces, registry, concurrency gating
│   ├── tmux.go       # TmuxSession for interactive PTY sessions
│   └── kiro.go       # Kiro agent (kiro-cli-chat)
├── entire/           # `entire` CLI wrapper
│   └── entire.go     # Enable, Disable, RewindList, Rewind
├── testutil/         # Shared test infrastructure
│   ├── metadata.go   # Checkpoint/session metadata types
│   ├── artifacts.go  # Artifact capture (git-log, pane, logs)
│   ├── repo.go       # RepoState, SetupRepo, ForEachAgent, Git helpers
│   └── assertions.go # Test assertions (testify-based)
├── bootstrap/        # Pre-test agent bootstrap (CI auth setup)
│   └── main.go       # go run ./e2e/bootstrap
├── setup_test.go     # TestMain: build agents, artifact dir, preflight
├── kiro_lifecycle_test.go  # Lifecycle tests (ForEachAgent pattern)
├── kiro_test.go      # Protocol-level tests (stdin/stdout subcommands)
├── harness.go        # AgentRunner for protocol tests
├── testenv.go        # TestEnv for protocol tests
└── fixtures.go       # HookInput, KiroTranscript builders
```

## Running Tests

### All E2E tests (protocol + lifecycle)

```bash
make test-e2e
```

### Lifecycle tests only

```bash
make test-e2e-lifecycle
```

### Single test

```bash
cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 -run TestLifecycle_SinglePromptManualCommit ./...
```

func CaptureArtifacts(t *testing.T, s *RepoState) {
    dir := s.ArtifactDir  // Set during SetupRepo()
    
    // Git state
    writeArtifact(t, dir, "git-log.txt", gitOutput(...))
    writeArtifact(t, dir, "git-tree.txt", gitOutput(...))
    
    // Interactive session pane
    if s.session != nil {
        writeArtifact(t, dir, "pane.txt", s.session.Capture())
    }
    
    // Status marker
    writeArtifact(t, dir, "PASS" or "FAIL", "")
    
    // Checkpoint metadata from checkpoint branch
    captureCheckpointMetadata(t, s, dir)
    
    // Entire logs from .entire/logs/
    captureEntireLogs(t, s.Dir, dir)
    
    // Preserved repo symlink (if E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1)
    os.Symlink(s.Dir, filepath.Join(dir, "repo"))
}
```

# Debug Entire CLI via E2E Artifacts

Diagnose Entire CLI bugs using captured artifacts from the E2E test suite. Artifacts are written to `e2e/artifacts/` locally or downloaded from CI via GitHub Actions.

## Inputs

The user provides either:
- **A test run directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/` -- triage all failures
- **A specific test directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/{TestName}-{agent}/` -- debug one test

## Artifact Layout

```
e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/
├── report.nocolor.txt          # Pass/fail/skip summary with error lines
├── test-events.json            # Raw Go test events (NDJSON)
├── entire-version.txt          # CLI version under test
└── {TestName}-{agent}/
    ├── PASS or FAIL            # Status marker
    ├── console.log             # Full operation transcript
    ├── git-log.txt             # git log --decorate --graph --all
    ├── git-tree.txt            # ls-tree HEAD + checkpoint branch
    ├── entire-logs/entire.log  # CLI structured JSON logs
    ├── checkpoint-metadata/    # Checkpoint + session metadata
    └── repo -> /tmp/...        # Symlink to preserved repo (E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1 only)
```

## Preserved Repo

When the test run was executed with `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1`, each test's artifact directory contains a `repo` symlink pointing to the preserved temporary git repository. This is the actual repo the test operated on -- you can inspect it directly.

**Navigate via the symlink** (e.g., `{artifact-dir}/repo/`) rather than resolving the `/tmp/...` path. The symlink lives inside the artifact directory so permissions and paths stay consistent.

The preserved repo contains:
- Full git history with all branches (main, `entire/checkpoints/v1`)
- The `.entire/` directory with CLI state, config, and raw logs
- The `.claude/` directory (if Claude Code was the agent)
- All files the agent created or modified, in their final state

This is the most powerful debugging tool -- you can run `git log`, `git diff`, `git show`, inspect `.entire/` internals, and see exactly what the CLI left behind.

## Debugging Workflow

### 1. Triage (if given a run directory)

Read `report.nocolor.txt` to identify failures and their error messages. Each entry shows the test name, agent, duration, and failure output with file:line references.

### 2. Read console.log (most important)

Full transcript of every operation:
- `> claude -p "..." ...` -- agent prompts with stdout/stderr
- `> git add/commit/...` -- git commands
- `> send: ...` -- interactive session inputs

This tells you what happened chronologically.

### 3. Read test source code

Use the file:line from the report to find the test in `e2e/tests/`. Understand what the test expected to happen vs what console.log shows actually happened.

### 4. Diagnose the CLI behavior

Cross-reference console.log (what happened) with the test (what should have happened). Focus on CLI-level issues:

| Symptom | CLI Investigation |
|---------|-------------------|
| Checkpoint not created / timeout | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for hook invocations, phase transitions, errors |
| Wrong checkpoint content | Check `git-tree.txt` for checkpoint branch files, `checkpoint-metadata/` for session info |
| Hooks didn't fire | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for missing hook entries (session-start, user-prompt-submit, stop, post-commit) |
| Stash/unstash problems | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for stash-related log lines, `git-log.txt` for commit ordering |
| Attribution issues | Check `checkpoint-metadata/` for `files_touched`, session metadata for attribution data |
| Strategy mismatch | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for `strategy` field, verify auto-commit vs manual-commit behavior |

### 5. Deep dive files

- **entire-logs/entire.log**: Structured JSON logs -- hook lifecycle, session phases (`active` -> `idle` -> `ended`), warnings, errors. Key fields: `component`, `hook`, `strategy`, `session_id`.
- **git-log.txt**: Commit graph showing main branch, `entire/checkpoints/v1`, checkpoint initialization.
- **git-tree.txt**: Files at HEAD vs checkpoint branch (separated by `--- entire/checkpoints/v1 ---`).
- **checkpoint-metadata/**: `metadata.json` has `checkpoint_id`, `strategy`, `files_touched`, `token_usage`, and `sessions` array. Session subdirs have per-session details.

### 6. Report findings

Identify whether the issue is in:
- **CLI hooks** (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit)
- **Session management** (phase transitions, session tracking)
- **Checkpoint creation** (branch management, metadata writing)
- **Attribution** (file tracking, prompt correlation)
- **Strategy logic** (auto-commit vs manual-commit behavior)

# Debug External Agent E2E Failures

Diagnose external agent bugs using captured artifacts from the E2E test suite. Artifacts are written to `e2e/artifacts/` locally or downloaded from CI via GitHub Actions.

## Inputs

The user provides either:
- **A test run directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/` -- triage all failures
- **A specific test directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/{TestName}-{agent}/` -- debug one test

## Test Categories

This repo has two kinds of tests:

| Category | File | What it tests |
|----------|------|---------------|
| **Protocol tests** | `e2e/kiro_test.go` | Individual agent subcommands (`info`, `detect`, `get-session-id`, `parse-hook-event`, etc.) via `AgentRunner` |
| **Lifecycle tests** | `e2e/kiro_lifecycle_test.go` | Full workflows: prompt execution → git commit → checkpoint creation, using `entire` CLI + agent CLI |

Protocol tests run against the agent binary directly. Lifecycle tests require both the `entire` CLI and the agent's underlying CLI (e.g., `kiro-cli-chat`).

## Artifact Layout

```
e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/
├── entire-version.txt          # CLI version under test
└── {TestName}-{agent}/         # e.g., TestLifecycle_SinglePromptManualCommit-kiro
    ├── PASS or FAIL            # Status marker
    ├── console.log             # Full operation transcript
    ├── pane.txt                # Tmux pane capture (interactive session tests only)
    ├── git-log.txt             # git log --decorate --graph --all
    ├── git-tree.txt            # ls-tree HEAD + entire/checkpoints/v1 branch
    ├── entire-logs/entire.log  # CLI structured JSON logs from .entire/logs/
    ├── checkpoint-metadata/    # Checkpoint + session metadata
    │   └── {first-2-chars}/{remaining-10-chars}/
    │       ├── metadata.json   # Checkpoint-level metadata
    │       └── 0/
    │           └── metadata.json   # Session 0 metadata
    └── repo -> /tmp/...        # Symlink to preserved repo (E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1 only)
```

## Preserved Repo

When the test run was executed with `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1`, each test's artifact directory contains a `repo` symlink pointing to the preserved temporary git repository. This is the actual repo the test operated on -- you can inspect it directly.

**Navigate via the symlink** (e.g., `{artifact-dir}/repo/`) rather than resolving the `/tmp/...` path. The symlink lives inside the artifact directory so permissions and paths stay consistent.

The preserved repo contains:
- Full git history with all branches (main, `entire/checkpoints/v1`)
- The `.entire/` directory with CLI state, config, and raw logs
- All files the agent created or modified, in their final state

This is the most powerful debugging tool -- you can run `git log`, `git diff`, `git show`, inspect `.entire/` internals, and see exactly what the CLI left behind.

## Debugging Workflow

### 1. Triage (if given a run directory)

List the test subdirectories and check for `FAIL` markers. Read the Go test output or `console.log` files to identify failures and their error messages.

### 2. Read console.log (most important)

Full transcript of every operation:
- `> kiro-cli-chat chat --no-interactive ...` -- agent prompts with stdout/stderr
- `> git add/commit/...` -- git commands
- `> send: ...` -- interactive session inputs (tmux-based tests)

This tells you what happened chronologically.

### 3. Read test source code

Find the failing test in `e2e/kiro_lifecycle_test.go` (lifecycle tests) or `e2e/kiro_test.go` (protocol tests). Understand what the test expected vs what console.log shows actually happened.

Key test infrastructure to understand:
- `testutil.ForEachAgent()` -- runs the test for each registered agent with timeout
- `testutil.SetupRepo()` -- creates temp git repo, runs `entire enable`, sets up artifact capture
- `s.RunPrompt()` -- sends a prompt to the agent and captures output
- `testutil.WaitForCheckpoint()` -- polls until checkpoint branch advances
- `testutil.CaptureArtifacts()` -- called during t.Cleanup(), writes all artifacts

### 4. Diagnose the issue

Cross-reference console.log (what happened) with the test (what should have happened). Determine whether the issue is in the agent, the CLI, or the test itself:

| Symptom | Investigation |
|---------|---------------|
| Agent subcommand returns wrong output | Protocol test: check `AgentRunner` invocation, compare expected vs actual JSON |
| Agent prompt fails / produces wrong files | Check `console.log` for agent stderr, verify agent CLI is available |
| Checkpoint not created / timeout | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for hook invocations, phase transitions, errors |
| Wrong checkpoint content | Check `git-tree.txt` for checkpoint branch files, `checkpoint-metadata/` for session info |
| Hooks didn't fire | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for missing hook entries (session-start, user-prompt-submit, stop, post-commit) |
| Detection fails | Check agent `detect` subcommand output, verify expected files exist in repo |
| Session ID wrong | Check `get-session-id` output, verify `.entire/` session state |
| Attribution issues | Check `checkpoint-metadata/` for `files_touched`, session metadata for attribution data |
| Strategy mismatch | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for `strategy` field, verify auto-commit vs manual-commit behavior |
| Interactive session hangs | Check `pane.txt` for tmux capture, look for agent waiting on input |

### 5. Deep dive files

- **console.log**: Chronological transcript of all operations. Most important file for understanding what happened.
- **pane.txt**: Tmux pane capture for interactive session tests. Shows the terminal state at artifact capture time.
- **entire-logs/entire.log**: Structured JSON logs -- hook lifecycle, session phases (`active` -> `idle` -> `ended`), warnings, errors. Key fields: `component`, `hook`, `strategy`, `session_id`.
- **git-log.txt**: Commit graph showing main branch, `entire/checkpoints/v1`, checkpoint initialization.
- **git-tree.txt**: Files at HEAD vs checkpoint branch (separated by `--- entire/checkpoints/v1 ---`).
- **checkpoint-metadata/**: `metadata.json` has `checkpoint_id`, `strategy`, `files_touched`, `token_usage`, and `sessions` array. Session subdirs have per-session details including `agent`, `transcript_path`, and `initial_attribution`.

### 6. Report findings

Identify whether the issue is in:
- **Agent binary** (protocol compliance, subcommand output, detection logic)
- **Agent CLI** (kiro-cli-chat behavior, prompt execution, file creation)
- **Entire CLI hooks** (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit)
- **Session management** (phase transitions, session tracking, session IDs)
- **Checkpoint creation** (branch management, metadata writing, content hash)
- **Attribution** (file tracking, prompt correlation, transcript capture)
- **Test harness** (testutil assertions, timing, environment setup)

# Debug Entire CLI via E2E Artifacts

Diagnose Entire CLI bugs using captured artifacts from the E2E test suite. Artifacts are written to `e2e/artifacts/` locally or downloaded from CI via GitHub Actions.

## Inputs

The user provides either:
- **A test run directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/` -- triage all failures
- **A specific test directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/{TestName}-{agent}/` -- debug one test

## Artifact Layout

```
e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/
├── report.nocolor.txt          # Pass/fail/skip summary with error lines
├── test-events.json            # Raw Go test events (NDJSON)
├── entire-version.txt          # CLI version under test
└── {TestName}-{agent}/
    ├── PASS or FAIL            # Status marker
    ├── console.log             # Full operation transcript
    ├── git-log.txt             # git log --decorate --graph --all
    ├── git-tree.txt            # ls-tree HEAD + checkpoint branch
    ├── entire-logs/entire.log  # CLI structured JSON logs
    ├── checkpoint-metadata/    # Checkpoint + session metadata
    └── repo -> /tmp/...        # Symlink to preserved repo (E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1 only)
```

## Preserved Repo

When the test run was executed with `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1`, each test's artifact directory contains a `repo` symlink pointing to the preserved temporary git repository. This is the actual repo the test operated on -- you can inspect it directly.

**Navigate via the symlink** (e.g., `{artifact-dir}/repo/`) rather than resolving the `/tmp/...` path. The symlink lives inside the artifact directory so permissions and paths stay consistent.

The preserved repo contains:
- Full git history with all branches (main, `entire/checkpoints/v1`)
- The `.entire/` directory with CLI state, config, and raw logs
- The `.claude/` directory (if Claude Code was the agent)
- All files the agent created or modified, in their final state

This is the most powerful debugging tool -- you can run `git log`, `git diff`, `git show`, inspect `.entire/` internals, and see exactly what the CLI left behind.

## Debugging Workflow

### 1. Triage (if given a run directory)

Read `report.nocolor.txt` to identify failures and their error messages. Each entry shows the test name, agent, duration, and failure output with file:line references.

### 2. Read console.log (most important)

Full transcript of every operation:
- `> claude -p "..." ...` -- agent prompts with stdout/stderr
- `> git add/commit/...` -- git commands
- `> send: ...` -- interactive session inputs

This tells you what happened chronologically.

### 3. Read test source code

Use the file:line from the report to find the test in `e2e/tests/`. Understand what the test expected to happen vs what console.log shows actually happened.

### 4. Diagnose the CLI behavior

Cross-reference console.log (what happened) with the test (what should have happened). Focus on CLI-level issues:

| Symptom | CLI Investigation |
|---------|-------------------|
| Checkpoint not created / timeout | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for hook invocations, phase transitions, errors |
| Wrong checkpoint content | Check `git-tree.txt` for checkpoint branch files, `checkpoint-metadata/` for session info |
| Hooks didn't fire | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for missing hook entries (session-start, user-prompt-submit, stop, post-commit) |
| Stash/unstash problems | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for stash-related log lines, `git-log.txt` for commit ordering |
| Attribution issues | Check `checkpoint-metadata/` for `files_touched`, session metadata for attribution data |
| Strategy mismatch | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for `strategy` field, verify auto-commit vs manual-commit behavior |

### 5. Deep dive files

- **entire-logs/entire.log**: Structured JSON logs -- hook lifecycle, session phases (`active` -> `idle` -> `ended`), warnings, errors. Key fields: `component`, `hook`, `strategy`, `session_id`.
- **git-log.txt**: Commit graph showing main branch, `entire/checkpoints/v1`, checkpoint initialization.
- **git-tree.txt**: Files at HEAD vs checkpoint branch (separated by `--- entire/checkpoints/v1 ---`).
- **checkpoint-metadata/**: `metadata.json` has `checkpoint_id`, `strategy`, `files_touched`, `token_usage`, and `sessions` array. Session subdirs have per-session details.

### 6. Report findings

Identify whether the issue is in:
- **CLI hooks** (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit)
- **Session management** (phase transitions, session tracking)
- **Checkpoint creation** (branch management, metadata writing)
- **Attribution** (file tracking, prompt correlation)
- **Strategy logic** (auto-commit vs manual-commit behavior)
